page: Microsoft Azure EC48as_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the ECasv6 server family. It is powered by an AMD processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance features 48 dedicated vCPUs and 384.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU.

This server configuration does not include any local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via external block storage. It is designated as Premium Storage capable, allowing integration with high-performance persistent disks. There are zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses bundled with this instance.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation and high memory-to-core ratio position this instance as a specialized resource for memory-intensive workloads rather than general-purpose compute. It is designed for workloads such as in-memory databases, large-scale caching layers, data analytics, and enterprise applications that require substantial memory capacity without the need for local physical disks or onboard GPUs.

The server does not include local storage but is Premium Storage capable, allowing for high-performance external disk configurations. With zero bundled public IPv4 addresses and no onboard GPUs, the hardware profile is tailored specifically for memory-intensive operations. It is highly suited for workloads such as in-memory databases, large-scale caching, data analytics, and enterprise applications requiring substantial RAM capacity.
